title: WALES MILLENNIUM CENTRE TOURS (ENGLISH)
description:
Take our backstage tour and get an exclusive behind-the-scenes glimpse into the world of theatre.

Beginning at our iconic Donald Gordon Theatre, you’ll be guided through our rarely seen backstage areas before ending your tour with an opportunity for photos by our famous inscription wall.

Afterwards, take your time to explore our front of house spaces, architecture and art, grab a seat and watch the world go by from our ground floor café-bar, Ffwrnais. Your delicious lunch, coffee or fresh pastry will taste all the better knowing you get 10% off by showing your tour ticket. You can also visit Armadilo, our theatre Shop, where you’ll also enjoy 10% off with your ticket—perfect for picking up a unique gift or souvenir.

We pride ourselves on being open and welcoming to everyone. If you have any specific access requirements, please contact us before the tour so that we can make any necessary arrangements.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Wales Millennium Centre Tours (English)

title: ANNIE
description:
The smash-hit West End production of Annie, the world’s favourite family musical, comes to Cardiff for 2 weeks only, starring Claire Sweeney as Miss Hannigan.

Set in 1930s New York, brave young Annie is forced to live a life of misery at Miss Hannigan’s orphanage. Her luck soon changes when she's chosen to spend a fairytale Christmas with famous billionaire, Oliver Warbucks. Meanwhile, spiteful Miss Hannigan has other ideas and hatches a plan to spoil Annie’s search for her true family...

With its Tony® award-winning book and score, including the unforgettable songs It’s The Hard-Knock Life, Easy Street, I Don’t Need Anything But You and Tomorrow, you can bet your bottom dollar that you’ll love it!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Annie

title: COWBOYS COMEDY
description:
Cardiff’s wildest comedy night is BACK.

The sold-out Wild West comedy cabaret returns for a rootin’-tootin’ night of chaos in the saloon. Expect gut-busting stand-up, sassy burlesque and fabulous drag in a no-rules, high-energy Wild West cabaret.

Hosted by bad-boy sheriff Cowboy Joe, Cowboys Comedy! is a high-energy, anything-goes night for outlaws, weirdos, and cowpokes who like their comedy a little bit unhinged.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > COWBOYS COMEDY

title: STEPH WEBBER - GLITTERING NIGHTS
description:
Join Ebbw Vale-born singer Steph Webber for an evening of music, storytelling, and sparkle in the intimate cabaret lounge at Cardiff Bay WMC.

A graduate of the Arden School of Theatre, Steph has built a successful international career performing on stages across the world, including headlining on luxury cruise liners with her own shows. Many will recognise her from The Voice UK, where she received one of the fastest chair turns from will.i.am and was mentored by Sir Tom Jones. She has appeared at the Cardiff New Theatre, starring opposite David Hasselhoff in Peter Pan, and has become a familiar face to local audiences after performing in pantomime there five times. She also appeared in the award-winning musical Tiger Bay at the Wales Millennium Centre.Glittering Nights showcases Steph’s powerful vocals, humour, and natural warmth in a relaxed cabaret setting, featuring well-loved songs from artists including Adele and Shirley Bassey, alongside musical theatre favourites and a touch of country. Accompanied live on grand piano by David Laugharne, this promises to be an engaging and entertaining evening of live music.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Steph Webber - Glittering Nights

title: SHOWGIRLS AND SHARKS
description:
Showgirls & Sharks: A madcap musical autobiography. The true story of how a young girl ran away with the circus and became a Welsh Burlesque legend!

Showgirls & Sharks follows fearless showgirl “FooFoo LaBelle” through an incredible musical re-telling of her life’s story.   Accompanied by musical Maestro Jenna Dyckhoff, the all-singing, all-dancing duo expose the secrets of a globe-trotting showgirl.

This funny, touching and sometimes heart-breaking quest for stardom will take you on a journey of self-discovery which spans five decades and three continents. You’ll Meet ballet dancers, circus elephants, performing sharks and pantomime cats in this unique evening of storytelling, song and cabaret.

A real-life musical adventure uncovers a true story of surviving womanhood, show-business and all the world has to offer before finding the true joys of home.  An unbelievable tale that will have you singing along, laughing out loud and wiping away tears as you discover that showgirls are like fine wine or smelly cheese: they only get better with age!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Showgirls and Sharks

title: THE BODYGUARD
description:
The international, award-winning smash-hit musical The Bodyguard is back!

Starring international leading actor Sidonie Smith* (Sister Act, Chicago, Jesus Christ Superstar) as Rachel Marron, Adam Garcia (Chitty Chitty Bang Bang, Wicked, Coyote Ugly) as Frank Farmer, and Matt Milburn (Coronation Street, Hollyoaks) as Sy Spector.

Former Secret Service agent turned bodyguard, Frank Farmer, is hired to protect superstar Rachel Marron from an unknown stalker. Each expects to be in charge – what they don't expect is to fall in love.

A ‘Brilliant!’ (The Times), breathtakingly romantic thriller, The Bodyguard features a whole host of irresistible classics including Queen of the Night, So Emotional, One Moment in Time, Saving All My Love, Run to You, I Have Nothing, Greatest Love Of All, Million Dollar Bill, I Wanna Dance With Somebody and one of the greatest hit songs of all time – I Will Always Love You.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Bodyguard

title: EVOLVER
description:
Explore the branching human body at the scale of a forest.  Evolver is a 360º audio visual world which is experienced through an audio meditation, on screen and in virtual reality.

Experience this transcendental art installation which makes visible the journey of breath as it flows through the landscape of the human body; from lungs to heart to cell. In following breath, we discover the world flows into us and we flow into the world. The artwork challenges the boundary between self and other, inside and outside, human and nature.

Narrated by Cate Blanchett with Music by Radiohead’s Jonny Greenwood, avant-garde artist Meredith Monk, late Icelandic composer Jóhann Jóhannsson and experimentalist Howard Skempton and spiritually informed ambient electronic maestro Jon Hopkins.

Evolver is rooted in scientific observation, using data gathered through extensive collaboration with leading scientific institutions. The resulting artwork places the audience as explorers at the outer limits of the observable. By exploring the human body at the size of a forest; universal patterns emerge.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Evolver

title: GLENDA & RITA
description:
Glenda & Rita are two actresses from the Golden Era of cinema, immortalised in black & white and struggling to find work in this brutal, unforgiving technicolour world.

The Silver Starlets will take you on a musical journey, as they sing the hits from their favourite movies throughout the decades, from ‘A Star Is Born’, to ‘Spice World’.This cinematic romp from Brighton’s Drag Idol Finalists is not to be missed!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Glenda & Rita

title: THE RAT PACK LAS VEGAS LIVE!
description:
The Official UK No: 1 Tribute show as voted by The Agents Association of Great Britain for three years running, comes to Wales Millennium Centre direct From London’s Leicester Square Theatre, The 02 and a Royal Command Performance.

The acclaimed David Alacey (Lovejoy & Inspector Alleyn) stars as Frank Sinatra alongside West End Favourite Tim Harwood as Dean Martin. They are joined by hit BBC show “Jamie Johnson FC” star Jim Whitley as Sammy Davis Jnr in the original Rat Pack show, now celebrating its 27th record breaking year. Together they amaze audiences with their recreation of the music of Sinatra, Martin and Davis, in a fabulous show which brings the style and excitement of the Las Vegas heyday to vivid life. Including all the great hits - My Way, Mr Bojangles, New York New York, Amore, Come Fly With Me! The show also features the BBC Musical Director Mac Shone at The Piano alongside the Buddy Greco All-Stars. Greco who was a member of the real Pack in Vegas and a friend of Marilyn Monroe (having spent the famous last weekend at Lake Tahoe with Sinatra and Marilyn) gave the show his seal of approval saying “These guys are as close as anyone will ever get to my dear friends, it was just like being back at The Sands” He also adds an authentic touch of Rat Pack style to the show, by making a specially recorded guest appearance. Just as Frank, Dean and Sammy were joined on stage at The Sands by surprise Special Guests, that element of theatrical magic is recreated in this show with appearances by the likes of Marilyn Monroe, Nancy Sinatra and Liza Minnelli adding even more glamour to this glitzy production. Memories really are made of this!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Rat Pack Las Vegas Live!

title: AN EVENING WITH SIR DAVID JASON
description:
Sir David Jason is delighted to be taking to the stage at Wales Millennium Centre to present his brand-new ‘An Evening with Sir David Jason’ show.

With a Welsh mother, who was born and raised in Merthyr Tydfil, Sir David is really looking forward to returning to South Wales, where he has so many happy memories of his time spent with his Welsh relatives over many, many years.

Through hilarious anecdotes and personal reflections, and joined on stage by Presenter Mike Bushell (BBC Breakfast), Sir David takes the audience on a journey—from his early days in amateur dramatics to becoming one of Britain’s most beloved actors starring in shows such as Only Fools and Horse, A Touch of Frost, Open All Hours and The Darling Buds of May. He reveals how chance encounters, hard work, and a little luck led him to create some of the most iconic TV characters in British history.

Discover how Del Boy’s character evolved beyond the writer’s original vision, how he crafted unique performances for his diverse roles, and how he brought legendary animated characters like Danger Mouse, The BFG, Count Duckula, and Toad to life—sometimes with just a single image as inspiration.

Relive some of the most unforgettable moments in British TV history, including the legendary Only Fools and Horses scenes—the Bar Fall, The Jolly Boys Outing, Batman and Robin, and more. David takes you behind the curtain, revealing the secrets, techniques, and on-set magic that made these moments so timeless.

Join Sir David Jason for an evening of nostalgia, laughter, and exclusive insights into a career that has left an indelible mark on British television.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > An Evening with Sir David Jason

title: COFFI A CYMRU
description:
Explore coffee's origins and rituals, and its cultural and historical connection to Wales.

Join Natalie, founder of Boss & Brew Academy and Hanan, the National Poet of Wales, for an interactive workshop blending gentle creative writing exercises with a Welsh coffee tasting experience.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Coffi a Cymru

title: WALES MILLENNIUM CENTRE TOURS (WELSH)
description:
Take our backstage tour and get an exclusive behind-the-scenes glimpse into the world of theatre.

Beginning at our iconic Donald Gordon Theatre, you’ll be guided through our rarely seen backstage areas before ending your tour with an opportunity for photos by our famous inscription wall.

Afterwards, take your time to explore our front of house spaces, architecture and art, grab a seat and watch the world go by from our ground floor café-bar, Ffwrnais. Your delicious lunch, coffee or fresh pastry will taste all the better knowing you get 10% off by showing your tour ticket. You can also visit Armadilo, our theatre Shop, where you’ll also enjoy 10% off with your ticket—perfect for picking up a unique gift or souvenir.

We pride ourselves on being open and welcoming to everyone. If you have any specific access requirements, please contact us before the tour so that we can make any necessary arrangements.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Wales Millennium Centre Tours (Welsh)

title: EXPLORE PLATFFORM: SUMMER SESSIONS
description:
EXPLORE PLATFFORM: SUMMER TASTERS

Find Tickets

Are you 11-14 and curious about music, writing, art, performance or radio? Platfform at Wales Millennium Centre does it all, and we’re opening our doors this summer for you to come and try it out!

Join us on 28, 29 and 30 July and take part in fun, hands-on workshops where you can create, perform and experiment.

You’ll meet new people, work with professional artists, meet our team and find out how to get involved at Platfform. No experience needed, just come along and give it a go!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Explore Platfform: Summer Sessions

title: THE KARATE KID
description:
The iconic 80s generation defining movie is now a dynamic new musical.

Daniel is the new kid in town trying to fit in until he becomes the target of Cobra Kai’s relentless star student, Johnny. Underestimated and outmatched, Daniel struggles to find his footing, until Ali, Johnny’s ex, sees something in Daniel that no one else does. As Johnny refuses to step aside, teenage rivalry turns into something more sinister. Daniel discovers guidance from Mr. Miyagi who teaches him that karate isn’t about fighting, it’s about balance, respect and that the greatest victories are won from within.

Featuring a Tony Award®-winning design team and an electrifying new score, this ‘elegant, emotional and remarkable’ (BroadwayWorld) coming-of-age masterpiece is reimagined with stunning artistry and groundbreaking choreography.

Honouring the roots of the timeless story, this musical celebrates resilience, mentorship, and the quiet strength that bridges generations.

Destiny is in your hands. Wax on. Wax off. Book now.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Karate Kid

title: NATIONAL OPEN YOUTH ORCHESTRA WITH BBC NATIONAL ORCHESTRA OF WALES
description:
Get ready for an afternoon that bursts with energy and invention. Eighteen brilliant disabled and non-disabled young musicians from the National Open Youth Orchestra (NOYO) join guest players from BBC NOW in a concert that redefines what a modern orchestra can be.

Expect a dazzling mix of instruments, from strings and brass to accordion, harp and electronic sounds, creating a soundworld that’s bold and fresh. Don’t miss their vivid performance of selections from Vivaldi’s The Seasons, and their joyful take on Florence Price’s Juba Dance, complete with swanee whistle! Michael Betteridge’s electrifying Soaring Through Sparks stars soloist Alessandro Vazzana, who plays the Clarion electronic instrument using head movement.

This will be a concert full of heart, with NOYO performing Oliver Cross’s Barriers, a moving tribute to disabled musicians. Under the theme #ThisIsOurOrchestra, everyone has a part to play, on stage and in the audience, as musicians unveil The Orchestra: A Young Person’s Guide by Charlotte Harding - a bold reimagining of Britten’s iconic piece co-commissioned with the BBC.

This relaxed performance is suitable for all, including neurodivergent and disabled audiences, and families. Join us before the performance starts to try the Clarion accessible instrument.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > National Open Youth Orchestra with BBC National Orchestra of Wales

title: QUARTEY & BLAYNEY
description:
Sketch comedy with Carwyn Blayney (BBC New Comedy Awards 2025) and Ekow Quartey (BBC's Amandaland). Quartey & Blayney present a load of different characters and sketches. Some weird, some relatable, all of them just for you.

Starring Carwyn Blayney (BBC New Comedy Awards 2025, winner of Welsh Unsigned Stand Up 2024, currently starring in S4C’s Ar Led) and Ekow Quartey (Currently starring as JJ in BBC’s Amandaland, Pedro in Becoming Elizabeth on Lionsgate+, and Mark in Smothered on Sky and a 2x Ian Charleson Nominee and Associate Artist at Shakespeare’s Globe)
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Quartey & Blayney

title: THE VAGUELY DEVIANT
description:
Forget snow. Forget tinsel. Forget Mariah lurking in the shadows…

This year, we’re binning off the baubles and diving headfirst into a sun-soaked, sweat-dripping, glitter-drenched Australian summer fantasy

Welcome to a chaotic collision of drag, live music, and unhinged festive rebellion as House of Deviant and Vaguely Artistic serve up a show that’s about as far from Christmas as you can possibly get.

Think:

The House of Deviant queens are bringing their signature anarchic drag brilliance—bigger, louder, and hotter than ever—while Vaguely Artistic soundtrack the madness with their unmistakable live, genre-smashing sound.

Expect outrageous performances, unexpected twists, ridiculous reimaginings of “festive” traditions, and more camp than a caravan park in the Outback.

This is not a Christmas show. This is a heatwave hallucination in heels.

So grab your sunnies, slap on the SPF (or glitter, same thing), and join us for a night of:

The Vaguely Deviant Not Even Remotely Christmas Australian Summer Show — because who said December had to be cold, cosy, or remotely appropriate?
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Vaguely Deviant

title: THE STARS OF TOMORROW
description:
Over 40 local theatre schools, singers, and groups unite for a spectacular showcase of youth performing arts.

Taking place over four consecutive evenings, this vibrant production celebrates the next generation of performers and will include solo singers, musical theatre and drama groups, street and hip-hop dancers, ballet dancers, cheerleaders, gymnasts, Irish dancers, ballroom & Latin, and a variety of tap, modern and jazz from the best local dance and drama schools.

With astounding young talent, dazzling lights, and stunning visual effects, this is truly a show not to be missed!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Stars of Tomorrow

title: OPEN OFFICE
description:
Are you a Theatre or Cabaret Artist looking to connect with Wales Millennium Centre?

Open Office is your opportunity to chat with us! We are mixing things up a little this year and giving you access to our Productions Producers and Cabaret Producer.

Victoria John - Senior Producer for Productions and Lauren Hughes - Assistant Producer for Productions

This is a chance for you to come and meet V and Lauren, chat about an idea, get advice or find out more about our Artist Development programme and creative opportunities.

This will be an informal chat over coffee in a relaxed environment. V and Lauren are keen to meet as many Theatre Artists as possible, to find out more about you and your practice, so come along!

Sessions with V and Lauren are: 28th August, 30th October and 27th November 2026 at 10am, 11.30am, 1.30pm or 3pm

Aled Lloyd Rees - Cabaret Producer

Do you have an idea for a cabaret show? Are you looking for advice or interested in developing work in cabaret?

Aled Lloyd Rees is hosting informal conversations as an opportunity to share ideas, ask questions and explore how your work could develop, all in a relaxed setting over a coffee.

Whether you are an emerging artist or already creating work, Aled is keen to connect, listen and support the growth of cabaret in Wales. Sessions with Aled are 7th August and 25th September 2026 at 10am, 11.30am, 1.30pm or 3pm.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Open Office

title: JERSEY BOYS
description:
The boys are back as the original Tony®-winning sensation returns for its 20th Anniversary Tour!

Go behind the music and inside the story of Frankie Valli and The Four Seasons in the true-life musical phenomenon, Jersey Boys. From the streets of New Jersey to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, this is the musical that’s too good to be true.

They were just four guys from Jersey, until they sang their very first note. They had a sound nobody had ever heard... and the radio just couldn’t get enough of.  But while their harmonies were perfect on stage, off stage it was a very different story – a story that has made them an international sensation all over again.

The show features all their hits including “Sherry,” “Big Girls Don’t Cry,” “Walk Like A Man,” “Can’t Take My Eyes Off You,” “Beggin’,”“Oh What a Night” and more.

The 20th Anniversary production boasts a stellar cast lead by Luke Baker (Everybody’s Talking About Jamie, Sheffield/West End;  Billy Elliot, Curve Leicester) as Frankie Valli, Carlo Boumouglbay (Muriel’s Wedding, Curve Leicester; Miss Saigon, international tour) as Tommy DeVito, Lewis Kennedy (All male Pirates of Penzance, Wilton’s Music Hall) as Nick Massi, Toby Miles (Les Misérables, West End;  School of Rock, international tour) as Bob Gaudio and Ellis Kirk (Dear Evan Hansen, West End/Asia tour) as Alternate Frankie Valli.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Jersey Boys

title: FLEETWOOD MAC'S 'RUMOURS' WITH THE TRANSATLANTIC ENSEMBLE
description:
The 'Transatlantic Ensemble' return by popular demand to Wales Millenium Centre, after past sellout shows here two years in a row and multiple sell-outs at Edinburgh Fringe Festival and right across the UK.

They will be performing Fleetwood Mac's iconic Rumours album in its entirety, plus loads of other well-known Fleetwood Mac classics. Experience one of the greatest albums of all time, lovingly and accurately recreated live, by this stunning ensemble of gifted young musicians and vocalists.

There is just something so evocative about the Rumours album – something that strikes a chord with every true music fan. It has soundtracked the lives of multiple generations.

If you are a fan of the album, this stunning performance by this incredible ensemble is quite simply something you cannot afford to miss. Be sure to bring your singing voice and prepare to be amazed, as this band are quite simply phenomenal!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Fleetwood Mac's 'Rumours' with the Transatlantic Ensemble

title: CALON
description:
The Urdd Youth Theatre in collaboration with Theatr Cymru present Calon.

A unique and contemporary musical by Caryl Parry Jones, Non Parry, Elan and Miriam Isaac that throws a new light on Caryl's classics and celebrates the wealth of songs that Caryl has composed over the years. The derelict mansion of Cae'r Lonydd has seen better days. A group of youngsters are thrown together with the challenge to breathe new life into the place. Despite their doubts and through smiles and tears, laughter and determination, there is a spark of hope that this might end up being the best summer ever. Until another visitor arrives at Cae'r Lonydd to shake things up and sets the ultimate challenge.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Calon

title: BURLESQUE
description:
After taking the West End by storm — where it became summer 2025’s biggest hit, selling out night after night — Burlesque A BIG New Musical is coming to Cardiff in September.

From executive producer Christina Aguilera and written by Steven Antin, Burlesque invites you to a club like no other: a hidden world beneath the city lights where sequins sparkle, seduction shimmers and lives can change in the flick of a feather boa.

When Ali arrives in New York searching for the mother she never knew, she stumbles upon a dazzling underground scene alive with music, movement, and mystery. Drawn into this intoxicating world, she begins to uncover her extraordinary voice — and finds an unexpected family of dreamers, misfits and schemers who will change her life forever.

Burlesque is a scintillating journey of self-discovery that absolutely sizzles, featuring powerhouse songs by Christina Aguilera, Sia and Diane Warren, alongside thrilling new numbers from Todrick Hall and Jess Folley.

Written by Steven Antin, who also wrote and directed the original 2010 Burlesque movie.

Executive produced by Christina Aguilera, bringing her star power, musical legacy and commitment to the show.

Songs by Christina Aguilera, Sia and Diane Warren, with new numbers by Todrick Hall and Jess Folley.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Burlesque

title: LINLEY HAMILTON QUINTET
description:
Trumpeter, composer and broadcaster Linley Hamilton has been a leading voice on the Irish jazz scene for over three decades.

Balancing an international performing career with work as a BBC jazz broadcaster and university lecturer, he has released six acclaimed albums — from Taylor Made (2010) through to Ginger’s Hollow (2023), featuring Adam Nussbaum and Mark Egan.

Following a First Class Masters and a PhD in jazz performance, Linley joined Ulster University as a lecturer, while continuing to perform widely. He has headlined the Cork Jazz Festival with his ambitious Making Other Arrangements mini-big band with strings, toured Ireland with his quintet, and appeared at London’s Pizza Express Soho.

In 2019, Linley and his wife Maggie founded their own venue, Magy’s Farm, which has since hosted more than 100 concerts featuring international artists including Larry Goldings Trio, Ari Hoenig, Enemy, Eddie Henderson and Joe Webb Trio. In recognition of his contribution to jazz, Linley was awarded an MBE in 2024.

Expect lyrical trumpet playing, inventive arrangements, and the depth of experience that comes from a lifetime immersed in jazz.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Linley Hamilton Quintet

title: GREEN FINGERS
description:
"Green Fingers" is a theatre experience created with and for people with profound and multiple learning disabilities (PMLD).

Created by Oshi’s World, Green Fingers uses multi-sensory storytelling to take audiences on an immersive gardening adventure in a relaxed, responsive environment where audiences can engage with each element in their own way.

Touring in 2026, this production champions inclusive, meaningful theatrical experiences for those within the PMLD community and hopes to provide an experience that is full of all the joy, magic and curiosity that nature has to offer.

If you would like to know more about Green Fingers as an audience member or organization, please contact us at hello@oshisworld.org
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Green Fingers

title: GINGER JOHNSON - SHOW PONY
description:
You’ve seen her in the Masterchef kitchen… You’ve seen her on the Drag Race runway… Now it's time to see her in her element: outrageous, untamed and completely off the reins.

Following the explosive success of her smash hit 2024 tour ‘Ginger Johnson Blows Off!’, the nation’s silliest sausage is back on the road with her brand new live spectacular: Show Pony!

Promising high camp, low morals, and double helpings of her trademark ridiculousness, Show Pony is a barnstorming night of whip-smart comedy and top-tier talent that proves why Ginger is one of Britain’s most beloved queens.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Ginger Johnson - Show Pony

title: JIVE ACES
description:
Got the blues? Well, step right up, because The Jive Aces are bringing their swingin' show to town, and it’s gonna' flip your frown upside down! When these cats burst onto the stage in their signature yellow suits, you’ll be tapping your feet and grinning like a fool before you know it. This is more than just a show, it’s a proper good time packed with the hottest music from sizzling swing to the raucous roots of rock 'n' roll, that’ll leave you buzzing.

For 30 years they've have been taking their infectious tunes to over 40 countries and continuously proving why they are the UK’s No.1 jive and swing band. They effortlessly transition from smooth-as-butter ballads to rip-roaring rhythm & blues. They’ve played for the Queen, packed out the Royal Albert Hall, and made history as the first band to get to the Britain’s Got Talent finals. With 250 gigs a year, they’ve got a knack for turning any crowd into a dancing, cheering frenzy

You’ve probably caught their rendition of the Morecambe and Wise classic "Bring Me Sunshine" - a banger with nearly 4 million YouTube views that’s so upbeat a U.S. doctor prescribed it to chase away the blues. It’s been all over Strictly Come Dancing, Netflix, an American cop show and recently comedian Miranda Hart even declared it one of her favourite songs and stated on BBC Radio 3, "The Jive Aces will get me up off the floor and make me lose any self-consciousness and dance!

Their latest album, Keeping The Show On The Road, is stuffed with crowd favourites that hit No.1 on Mike Read’s Heritage Chart and got spins on BBC and Boom Radio. Those tracks, plus their classic hits, will have you moving from the first note.

These guys and gals are the real deal. They’ve shared stages with legends like Van Morrison, John Travolta, Priscilla Presley, Jamie Cullum, Toto and Status Quo, and nabbed awards like the City of Derry International Music Award and the Variety Club of Great Britain Award for their music and charity work. Their music videos? So sharp they’ve won film festival prizes. But what makes The Jive Aces special is how they make you feel like you’re right in the heart of the party.

Don’t miss the "Feelin’ Happy" Tour! Grab your dancing shoes and get ready for a night of swing, jive, and pure, unfiltered entertainment that’ll send you home with more uplifted than a hot air balloon race. Snag your tickets now for a show that’ll keep you smiling for days!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Jive Aces

title: GALIFREY CABARET
description:
The queerest TARDIS in space and time!

Hop aboard GALLIFREY CABARET for a spectacular night of cosmic drag and variety, all inspired by the greatest TV show in the universe – DOCTOR WHO!

This cult hit is coming to Cardiff. Featuring drag, stand-up, burlesque, music, and a vortex of other surprises, this huge cabaret celebrates being queer in the wild, mad, and wonderful Whoniverse.

The show is bold, inclusive, joyful, punk, and unapologetically queer – we’re committed to showcasing a broad and inclusive range of artists and performances.

The shows are hosted by Reece Connolly as the Doctor, with performances by Carrot (usually appearing as Donna Noble in drag) and special guest performers, all bringing characters from the Whoniverse to life.

Since our first show in November 2021, we’ve performed regularly at the Royal Vauxhall Tavern in London, and more recently moved to a new home at The Clapham Grand. The show has toured across the UK, including Cardiff, Brighton, and Manchester.

Please note: this is an unofficial tribute show and is not affiliated with the BBC or any other large, scary corporations.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Galifrey Cabaret

title: IN MEMORY OF AMY
description:
Anna Aarons' dedicated show in memory of the late, great Amy Winehouse celebrates its 10 year anniversary this year, as well as commemorating 15 years since the legend's passing.

Not a 'typical' tribute, Aarons', joined by her 5 piece band, takes audiences on a thoughtful journey of Winehouse's catalogue, her musical influences, as well as sewing in her original music; portraying Winehouse's influence on modern songwriting. After multiple sold out shows across Central London, Aarons is delighted to bring this highly regarded show to the Cabaret Millenium Centre.

Vocals - Anna AaronsKeys - Gareth BucklandGuitar - Magnus EricsonBass - Mehmet RaifDrums - Will JacksonSax - Kerry Williams
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > In Memory of Amy

title: TOM WARD
description:
Before Tom bails out on the modern world entirely to commit to nostalgia full-time, the burner phone using, Discman carrying, 1988-1995 obsessed freak is giving the present tense one last try. Not for him, but for his 2-year-old son who lives there. But how does 2026 compare to the one he grew up in? Tom explores the head f**k of popular culture in the modern world versus that of his youth in his trademark bittersweet style.

As seen on Live at the Apollo, Roast Battle and Qi.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Tom Ward

title: GIANT STEPS
description:
AN IMPROV COMEDY SHOW… WITH A BRILLIANT LIVE BAND!

Improvisation meets improvisation as hilarious UK improv comedians and virtuoso jazz musicians inspire each other in real time into leaps of comedy and creativity.

Created by comedians Max Dickins and Conor Jatter, Giant Steps features a rotating ensemble of talent from big name improv shows like Showstoppers and Austentatious, plus the best up-and-coming music talent.

Expect fresh licks and big laughs in an incomparable show that’s different every night.

GIANT STEPS IS A ONE-OF-A-KIND LIVE EXPERIENCE!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > GIANT STEPS

title: WELSH NATIONAL OPERA: BLUEBEARD’S CASTLE + OEDIPUS REX
description:
In this powerful double bill, two extraordinary works of the 20th century are brought together in a shared world of ritual, mystery and psychological intensity.

In Bluebeard’s Castle, Judith leaves everything behind to be with the enigmatic Duke Bluebeard. She soon discovers his home is filled with locked doors and as she demands each one be opened, she uncovers not only the secrets of his past, but something far more unsettling. As darkness gathers, love and fear become impossible to separate.

In the second half, the same stark setting becomes the ancient city of Thebes for Oedipus Rex. A plague grips the land, and its king is determined to uncover the truth behind it. As his search intensifies, he is forced to confront a destiny he cannot escape. What begins as a quest for answers, becomes a devastating revelation.

Gothic, atmospheric and visually striking, this unified staging draws powerful connections between two timeless myths.

Featuring music by Hildegard Von Bingen Conductor Lydia Yankovskaya Director Joe Hill-Gibbins

Cast Nathan Berg, Peter Hoare, Christine Rice

Original production of Bluebeard’s Castle English National Opera
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Welsh National Opera: Bluebeard’s Castle + Oedipus Rex

title: MISCAST CABARET
description:
The wrong roles. The right songs. The ultimate divas.

What happens when the casting is completely wrong… but the performances are absolutely right?

Welcome to Miscast Cabaret – ICONIC, a night of powerhouse vocals, unexpected roles and unapologetic diva energy.

Think songs you love  -  performed by the people you’d never expect to sing them.

It’s bold. It’s camp. It’s a little bit chaotic. And it’s all completely intentional.

Expect big voices, bigger personalities and a celebration of iconic songs done verydifferently.

Come for the chaos. Stay for the talent.

Featuring incredible local Welsh talent  - completely homegrown.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Miscast Cabaret

title: ESSENTIAL CHER
description:
Prepare to be dazzled by Trisha McCluney, star of BBC1’s “I Can See Your Voice” and one of the fastest-rising tribute artists.

As Essentially Cher, Trisha brings the magic of the legendary Cher to life, delivering a breathtakingly authentic experience.

Fresh off the back of her Australian tour with Las Vegas show "Legends in Concert", Trisha is coming to the Wales Millennium Centre on Fri 25th Sept to deliver her World class tribute to the Goddess of Pop. Trisha's ability to deliver every nuance of Cher's presence, from her striking resemblance to her powerful vocals and not forgetting her signature wit and sass is so flawlessly accurate that audiences consistently say it feels as though they are watching the real thing!This must-see show features an electrifying setlist of Cher’s greatest hits, including:
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Essential Cher

title: PROTEST, POWER, REVOLUTION, RESISTANCE
description:
Protest, Power, Revolution, Resistance is a concert with something urgent to say.

Conducted by Johanna Malangré, current Music Director of the Orchestre de Picardie in France, the programme showcases her electrifying musicality and imaginative leadership.

It begins with Joan Tower’s Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman, a brilliant, bracing piece for brass and percussion inspired by Copland’s famous fanfare but reimagined as a celebration of women who lead, risk, and transform the world. From there, the orchestra moves into the powerful sound world of Margaret Bonds’ The Montgomery Variations, written in response to the civil rights movement and shaped by Bonds’ experience of Montgomery, Alabama. Both reflective and resolute, its movements capture the collective determination of protest and the sense of a new beginning.

After the interval, Haydn’s Farewell Symphony reminds us that messages in music are not always shouted, sometimes they are staged with quiet ingenuity. What begins as a tense, impassioned symphony becomes a theatrical plea for compassion, as players famously leave the stage one by one. Finally, Shostakovich’s Ninth Symphony turns expectations upside down. Written in 1945, when a monumental victory symphony was demanded, Shostakovich produced instead a compact, spirited, and unpredictably shadowed work.

REFLECTIVE · COURAGEOUS · IMAGINATIVE

Joan Tower Fanfare for the Uncommon Woman Bonds The Montgomery Variations Haydn Symphony No.45 (Farewell Symphony) Interval Shostakovich Symphony No.9
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Protest, Power, Revolution, Resistance

title: WELSH NATIONAL OPERA: LA BOHÈME
description:
In the Latin Quarter of Paris, four young artists live for the moment, sharing what little they have; chasing big dreams and laughing through the cold.

When Rodolfo meets Mimì, a quiet seamstress with a fading light, their connection is instant and unforgettable. But in a world shaped by poverty and passion, love burns brightly but all too briefly.

Puccini’s deeply moving masterpiece captures the passion of youth through some of the most beautiful and recognisable music ever written. La bohème evokes the spirit and intensity of bohemian life: the joy of falling in love, the pain of letting go, and the fragile of it all.

This acclaimed production reimagines this beloved story with striking clarity and emotional power. Set in a stark, monochrome world inspired by 1930s Paris where shadows linger and the presence of death is never far away.

The result is a La bohème that feels both timeless and contemporary. It paints a vivid portrait of young lives lived on the edge, where every moment matters and every goodbye cuts deep.

wno.org.uk/boheme

Original production Glyndebourne

Conductor Corinna NiemeyerDirector Floris Visser

Rodolfo Carlos Cardoso

Mimì Alexia Voulgaridou
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Welsh National Opera: La bohème

title: WELSH NATIONAL OPERA: SCHOOLS CONCERT
description:
Welsh National Opera’s Schools Concert is back with a vibrant and welcoming introduction to live orchestral music for pupils from years three to seven.

With a theme of Roman and Greek mythology, hosted by Elin Llwyd, this free concert blends well-known hits with rarer pieces, all brought to life by WNO Orchestra and a group of outstanding soloists. Offenbach’s energetic Can-Can brings a sense of joy and familiarity, encouraging young audiences to connect straight away. Meanwhile, Beethoven’s music from Prometheus stands out with its bold, cinematic quality – almost like a superhero soundtrack – making it both accessible and thrilling, even for those new to classical music.

Adding to the playful atmosphere is Ballo de Orsi (‘Dance of the Bears’), a piece that feels wonderfully visual and full of character. Its lively narrative quality invites children to imagine scenes and movement, making the listening experience fun and immersive.

With its mythological theme, engaging repertoire and relaxed setting, this concert aims to create a memorable experience for the children attending, encouraging a passion for music and creativity.

The concert is designed to support the Curriculum for Wales and National Curriculum in England and is augmented by a free resource pack for teachers to support classroom learning before and after the event.

The concerts in Cardiff and Llandudno will be presented in the Welsh language.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Welsh National Opera: Schools Concert

title: WELSH NATIONAL OPERA: FAMILY CONCERT
description:
Welsh National Opera’s Family Concert is back with a vibrant and welcoming introduction to live orchestral music.

Hosted by Elin Llwyd, the concert blends well-known favourites brought to life by WNO Orchestra, alongside a group of outstanding soloists performing recognisable pieces for all the family.  Offenbach’s energetic Can-Can brings a sense of joy and familiarity, encouraging young audiences to connect straight away while Beethoven’s music from Prometheus stands out with its bold, cinematic quality almost like a superhero soundtrack, making it both accessible and thrilling, even for those new to classical music.

Adding to the playful atmosphere is Ballo de Orsi (‘Dance of the Bears’), a piece that feels wonderfully visual and full of character. Its lively, almost storytelling quality, invites children to imagine scenes and movement, making the listening experience both fun and immersive.

With its mythological theme, engaging repertoire and relaxed setting, this concert offers something enjoyable and memorable for everyone.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Welsh National Opera: Family Concert

title: HANNAH HORTON
description:
Stories on the Wind is the new live show from award winning saxophonist, composer and Selmer Artist Hannah Horton.

Named by Jazzwise as one to watch in 2026, Hannah brings her deeply personal new album to the stage with a quartet built on lyrical melody, groove, warmth and musical storytelling.

The show moves between intimate reflection and rhythmic energy, with Hannah’s distinctive sound on tenor and baritone saxophones at its heart. This is music shaped by memory, connection, hope and the quiet stories that stay with us. Spacious, melodic and deeply human, Stories on the Wind invites audiences into Hannah’s most personal musical world yet.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > HANNAH HORTON

title: MICHAEL AKADIRI: DON’T CALL ME UNCLE!
description:
Following two hit UK tours, Biggest Award in Comedy nominee and resident (junior) Doctor Michael Akadiri prescribes Cardiff with a potent dose of his latest offering, Don’t Call Me Uncle!

Multi-award-winning comedian Michael is proud to wear many hats: doctor, husband & father. Evidently, that list does not include uncle.

Yet, despite only being in his early 30s, “the supremely talented and likeable” (Metro) doctor, for the first time ever, was recently called uncle in public (a term usually reserved for elders!). This has sent him spiralling!

Join the ”‘hugely entertaining” (Chortle) Akadiri as he fights this lurid allegation, contemplates the difference between perception and reality all whilst keeping the laughs flowing with his trademark “sharp-wit” (Chortle) & “politically punchy” (Guardian) anecdotes.

As seen on Comedy Central LIVE, ITVs Stand Up Sketch & Channel 4’s Stand Up to Cancer. As heard on Capital Xtra’s Breakfast Show, LBC & BBC Radio 4’s Loose Ends Tour support for Jason Manford.

Winner - Komedia New Comedy AwardWinner - Comedian of Medicine Award, Haags Comedy FestivalNextUp Biggest Award in Comedy nominee
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Michael Akadiri: Don’t Call Me Uncle!

title: KATHERINE JENKINS 25 YEAR ANNIVERSARY TOUR
description:
The biggest selling classical artist of this century, Katherine Jenkins OBE, has today announced a brand new 18-date UK tour for 2026, to celebrate her incredible 25 Years in music.

The 25 Year Anniversary Tour is the first tour for Katherine in five years and is set to feature all her biggest hits including ‘Time To Say Goodbye’, the emotional ‘Jealous of the Angels’ and  ‘I Will Always Love You' and more.

Speaking on what it means to celebrate 25 years, Katherine says: “Reaching twenty-five years in music is incredibly emotional, but this tour is truly a celebration of the fans who have been there from the very beginning. To be heading out across the UK and Ireland for eighteen special shows feels less like a celebration of a career and more like a reunion with old friends and I can’t wait to stand on stage, look out into those familiar faces, and share it all over again.”

Katherine Jenkins OBE is officially the world’s most successful classical singer, having been named The Biggest Selling Classical Artist of the Century by Classic FM and earning a record-breaking 14 number-one albums since signing to Universal Classics at just 22. She burst onto the music scene in 2003 with a landmark performance at Westminster Cathedral honouring Pope John Paul II, followed by appearances at major international sporting events and at the Sydney Opera House.

A favourite of the British Royal Family, Jenkins has sung at many national occasions including Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II’s Diamond Jubilee, Coronation concerts at Buckingham Palace, and her 90th birthday celebrations at Windsor Castle. She has also performed for Popes, Presidents and Princes, further establishing her place as one of the world’s most in-demand classical performers.

Alongside her musical achievements, Katherine has earned ‘National Treasure” status through her charitable work. She serves as global patron of The Wilderness Foundation, ambassador for Prince William’s TUSK Trust and Macmillan Cancer Support, and trustee of The British Forces Foundation - for whom she has performed for troops in conflict zones around the world. Awarded an OBE in 2013, she also lifted spirits during the 2020 pandemic with 25 free “lockdown live” concerts, including history-making performances at Buckingham Palace and a behind-closed-doors Royal Albert Hall concert.

A versatile creative force, Katherine has performed and recorded with many of the world’s leading artists, made her West End debut in Carousel to critical acclaim, and earned television recognition with appearances in Doctor Who, Dancing with the Stars (runner-up), and as a presenter on Songs of Praise. Her recent ventures include roles in films Minamata and Dream Horse, becoming Lady Sponsor of HMS Cardiff, launching her award-winning luxury gin brand Cygnet, and opening the inaugural SXSW London festival.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Katherine Jenkins 25 Year Anniversary Tour

title: JASDEEP SINGH DEGUN X BBC NOW
description:
Voices from across musical worlds come together as the BBC National Orchestra and Chorus of Wales joins composer‑sitarist Jasdeep Singh Degun for a powerful mix of Indian classical tradition and Western orchestral sound.

Featuring acclaimed soloists, reimagined operatic moments, and vibrant new works, this performance celebrates connection, creativity, and the many forms a voice can take.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Jasdeep Singh Degun X BBC NOW

title: AN EVENING WITH LIVERPOOL LEGEND IAN RUSH MBE
description:
An Evening With Liverpool Legend Ian Rush MBE

The ultimate evening of Liverpool legend Ian Rush live on stage hosted by Jed Stone. The evening will be full of entertainment and banter as you can well expect, so sit back and enjoy!

This evening will give you a full insight into his career with two spells at Liverpool becoming the highest goal scorer in history of the club, winning countless trophies along the way.

There will be limited opportunities for Meet and Greet pre-show, come along to meet Ian and have a professional photo with him.  Don’t miss out on your chance to be one of those…
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > An Evening With Liverpool Legend Ian Rush MBE

title: THE ULTIMATE CLASSIC ROCK SHOW
description:
Dust off your air guitars for an evening of the very best classic rock anthems from legends past and present.

Let The Ultimate Classic Rock Show take you on a roller coaster journey of classic rock nostalgia, performing hit after hit live with stunning accuracy and high energy accompanied by an incredible light and projection show, they're guaranteed to have you on your feet all night.

Over two hours of classic rock anthems from artists including Eric Clapton, Led Zeppelin, Queen, Pink Floyd, ELO, Deep Purple, Free, AC/DC, The Eagles and Jimi Hendrix to name but a few!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Ultimate Classic Rock Show

title: ROBERT WHITE
description:
Is going on tour with a brand new show!

Whether you’re a fan from his series of Britain’s Got Talent, or you’re one of his millions of online viewers but only just realising that Robert is ‘THAT’ Gay Autistic Keyboard Tanktop Comedy Guy, you’ll want to see his mix of songs, stories, stand up, gags, props, improvisation, interaction and all sorts of other engaging naughty nonsense besides!
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > ROBERT WHITE

title: DOG MAN: THE MUSICAL
description:
Dog Man: The Musical is a hilarious new musical based on the worldwide bestselling book series from Dav Pilkey, the creator of Captain Underpants and Cat Kid Comic Club.

Best friends George and Harold have been creating comics for years, but now that they’re ten, they figure it’s time to level up and write a musical based on their favourite character, Dog Man, the crime-biting sensation who is part dog, part man and ALL HERO! How hard could it be?

With the head of a dog and the body of a policeman, Dog Man loves to fight crime… and chew on the furniture.

But while trying his best to be a good boy, can he save the city from Flippy the cyborg fish and his army of Beasty Buildings? Can he catch Petey, the world’s most evil cat, who has cloned himself to exact revenge on the doggy do-gooder? And will George and Harold finish their show before lunchtime?

@dogmanthemusicaluk

IMAGES REGISTERED TRADEMARK OF DAV PILKEY.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Dog Man: The Musical

title: MIDGITTE BARDOT'S PERSONAL SPOT
description:
Don’t miss the all-singing, sometime-dancing, spirited chaos of Midgitte Bardot and their special guests as Midgitte Bardot's Personal Spot comes to Cardiff for the very first time.

You can expect an evening of drag, performance art, radical weird hoo-ha, liberation from today’s costly world, and a showtune or two.

Midgitte - the hilarious, subversive drag alter-ego of the supremely talented performance artist Tamm Reynolds - has been performing across the UK for a decade now, everywhere from Wilderness Festival to the Royal Court Theatre to Liverpool's dive bars. They recently completed a sold-out run of solo show Shooting From Below at the Southbank Centre. In 2023, with producer and curator Willy Amott, they launched a regular interdisciplinary performance programme and good ol' knees-up in London. Now it's on the road.

Next stop: Cabaret Caerdydd!

title: THE ALTERNATIVE CABARET: MUSICALS
description: Songs you know, but not as you know them.

title: JAMES BARR
description:
Domestic abuse isn’t funny, but this show is.

Trailblazing comedian James Barr is going on tour around the UK with a bold, "genre-defying" (ArtsHub) hour of stand-up. A darkly hilarious reckoning with shame, survival and a pushy-wushy down the stairsy-wairsy.

Nominee for Best Show at the ISH Edinburgh Comedy Awards, NextUp’s Biggest Award in Comedy and the Queer Performers Award at the 2025 Edinburgh Fringe, Sorry I Hurt Your Son (Said My Ex To My Mum) was also named one of the best reviewed shows of Edinburgh Fringe.

title: ODYSSEY II 'AMPLIFIED & UNLEASHED'
description:
Live. Bold. Unleashed. This is Odyssey II.

Kinetic School of Performing Arts proudly returns with 'Odyssey II: Amplified & Unleashed' – a spectacular celebration of music, movement, and raw performance energy.

Featuring all Kinetic Theatre Schools, this electrifying production brings together outstanding young talent from across South Wales for one unforgettable showcase. From powerhouse vocals to breathtaking choreography, Odyssey II promises an evening bursting with passion, creativity, and show-stopping entertainment.

Prepare to be inspired as the stage comes alive with dynamic performances, vibrant storytelling, and the unstoppable spirit of the next generation of performers.

title: DIRTY DANCING
description:
Dirty Dancing returns to Cardiff as part of a UK and Irish tour. Exploding with heart-pounding music, breath-taking emotion and sensationally sexy dancing.

Seen by millions across the globe this worldwide smash hit tells the classic story of Baby and Johnny, two fiercely independent young spirits from different worlds, who come together in what will be the most challenging and triumphant summer of their lives.

See the hit film come to life before your eyes with this fantastic, emotional and triumphant live stage show.

The iconic story features the hit songs 'Hungry Eyes', ‘Hey! Baby’, ‘Do You Love Me?’ and the heart stopping ‘(I’ve Had) The Time Of My Life’, and an incredible cast of dancers.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Dirty Dancing

title: RACHEL FAIRBURN: VEXY BEASTS
description:
Hot on the heels of her critically acclaimed 2025 show Side Eye, Rachel Fairburn is back with new personalities and some old favourites for you to love and hate.

Expect nepo babies, middle class rock stars, terrifying mothers, biting social commentary and lots of big laughs.

Co-host of International smash hit podcast All Killa No Filla and as seen on Live at the Apollo (BBC Two), Alan Davies: As Yet Untitled (Dave), Richard Osman’s House of Games (BBC Two) and Russell Howard Hour Life Lesson (Sky).

Instagram: @rachelfairburn

X: @RachelFairburn

TikTok: @rachelfairburncomedy
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > RACHEL FAIRBURN: Vexy Beasts

title: A VAGUELY SPOOOOOKY HALLOWEEN
description:
You're summoned to a night of cabaret-tinged Halloween warm-up chaos: part gig, part glorious curveball.

Expect an eclectic set of originals, left field covers and a couple of Halloween classics, served with theatrical flair, an unpredictable MC and a touch of bedevilment.

We’ll be worshiping the most Halloween-y outfit, so come dressed to dismember! But be prepared...you never know what might be lurking around the corner...
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > A Vaguely SpooOooky Halloween

title: ABERFAN 60
description:
REMEMBRANCE · COMMUNITY · HOPE

Marking the 60 years since the Aberfan disaster, this concert is anchored by Paul Mealor’s newly commissioned From Darkness into Light: a concert requiem for hope, bringing together Mark Lewis Jones, Aberfan Schools Choirs, Ynysowen Male Voice Choir, and the BBC National Chorus of Wales in an act of remembrance. Framing this centrepiece is music that moves from urgency to hope: Anna Clyne’s This Moment flashes with restless energy; Haydn’s E minor “Mourning” Symphony (No. 44) drives forward with fierce intensity; Mozart offers weight and luminous calm in the Andante cantabile from the “Jupiter”; and Vaughan Williams’ Whitman-inspired Toward the Unknown Region sets out on a searching journey towards the horizon.

Artists:

Tim Redmond – ConductorMark Lewis Jones – Narrator Ynysowen Primary School & Ysgol Gymraeg Rhyd y Grug Combined ChoirsYnysowen Male Voice Choir BBC National Chorus of Wales

Repertoire:

Anna Clyne This MomentHaydn Movement 1 Symphony No.44 ‘Mourning’ Mozart Movement 2 “Andante Cantabile” from Symphony No.41 ‘Jupiter'Vaughan-Williams Toward the Unknown RegionInterval | EgwylPaul Mealor Aberfan: From Darkness to Light

title: GAGGED! A QUEER COMEDY NIGHT
description:
Cabaret & Little Wander presents GAGGED! - a Queer Comedy Night!

Get ready for a night of big laughs and bold voices as GAGGED brings the best of the best from the UK’s Queer stand-up circuit to Cardiff Bay!

Hosted by the fabulous Lee Peart, expect an evening packed with sharp wit, camp chaos, and unapologetically queer comedy.

Come out, grab a drink, and get GAGGED with laughter!

title: FRAMERATE: PULSE OF THE EARTH
description:
FRAMERATE: Pulse of the Earth’s hypnotic imagery bears witness to landscapes in flux. The impact of human behaviour and the immense force of nature unfolds around you across an array of screens. This is a space where your perspective may shift.

FRAMERATE: Pulse of the Earth is the first in the FRAMERATE series. The work reveals alterations caused by human-centred industry and the rhythms of the natural world; destruction, extraction, habitation, construction, harvests, growth, and erosion. Three-dimensional stories unfold across this multichannel installation. Hypnotic imagery surrounds you. Audio shifts through the space. Spring breaks, summer sun shines through the leaves as they turn amber and fall a second later. In a quaint English garden a pumpkin grows. A thousand tonnes of steel are crushed. Sand ebbs and flows while a cliff retreats. 268 cows are milked. 519 pints are drunk.

Created from thousands of daily 3D time-lapse scans of British landscapes, the work observes change on a scale impossible to see with the lens of traditional cameras.

This is not just an artwork. The data collected and presented by FRAMERATE is ground-breaking scientific research, containing empirical, measurable facts published that have since been published in NATURE.

"FRAMERATE: Pulse of the Earth manifests as art, meditation and science in concert. Our team 3D time-lapse recorded urban, rural and industrial sites changing over time. The resulting hypnotic imagery observes environmental change on a spatial and temporal scale. With these ground- breaking visuals, we have created a unique installation guided by principles of architecture, theatre, music and mindfulness. This work invites people to consider their relationships to time, the environment, their community, and to change—offering a new way of looking at the world and relating to the planet." - Director's Statement.

title: HARRIET KEMSLEY
description:
Harriet’s life has been in constant chaos for as long as she can remember. So what happens when things start to go right for the multi-award winning stand-up comedian?

Following her eye opening appearance on Last One Laughing UK, Harriet is back on tour with a new show about dating, mushrooms and how she’s trying her absolute best to be a bit of a floozy.

As seen and heard on Live at the Apollo, Would I Lie to You?, 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown and co-host of the wildly successful Single Ladies In Your Area podcast.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > HARRIET KEMSLEY

title: HALLOWEEN SPOOKTACULAR
description:
SPOOKY · THRILLING · FUN

Join us this Halloween for our much loved Halloween Spooktacular!Bring the whole family for an afternoon adventure packed with Halloween favourites and delightfully mysterious music. Expect spine tingling moments, big orchestral sound, and plenty of fun as the orchestra conjures up ghosts, goblins and grins. Dressing up is part of the fun, so pop on your best costume and join us for a fang tastic family celebration!

Artists:

Barbara Dragan - Conductor

Gordon Hamilton ThrillerStephen Schwartz Ted Ricketts  arr. Highlights from "Wicked" John Williams Devil's Dance from the Witches of EastwickRay Parker Bell arr. Paul Campbell Ghostbusters Theme
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Halloween Spooktacular

title: OWAIN & HENRY
description:
An epic story of power, national identity, and the price of silence.

In 1400, Owain Glyndŵr (Michael Sheen), a forgotten lord of a small northern land, is declared an outlaw by King Henry IV. Rather than surrendering to his fate, Owain rises in rebellion. As the spark of his revolt spreads across the border, Henry finds himself struggling to keep the throne and the two men find themselves on opposite sides of history; one fighting for survival, the other for control.

Written by Olivier Award-winning Welsh playwright Gary Owen (Iphigenia in Splott), Owain & Henry is an historical epic charged with poetic power. This ambitious new production brings to life one of Wales’ most defining stories, where the fight for the existence of a nation hangs in the balance.

Co-directed by Michael Sheen and Steffan Donnelly of Theatr Cymru.

A Wales Millennium Centre and Welsh National Theatre co-production.

title: LAURA LEXX: YO-YO TOUR
description:
In a 10 year marriage eventually your partner sees it all. Every bad idea. Every silly decision. Every unfiltered thought, and somehow, they still say they love you.

The real twist is that somewhere along the way, you have to believe them.

And maybe, just maybe.. you learn to love yourself too.

Fresh off the success of her critically acclaimed show ‘Slinky’ (Winner of Best Show at the Women in Comedy Festival and twice nominated for Best International Debut at the New Zealand International Comedy Festival) multi award-winning comedy star Laura Lexx is back with her brand new show ‘Yo-Yo’.

title: WAR AND PEACE
description:
ELEGY JOURNEY RESILIENCE

War and peace meet in music of remembrance, journeying, and hardwon joy. Ravel opens with Le Tombeau de Couperin, his 1917 suite reimagined for orchestra: Baroque dance forms refracted through glittering colour, sudden shifts of light and delicately layered sound.  Vaughan Williams then turns inward with Songs of Travel, sung here by baritone Steffan Lloyd Owen, tales of the wanderer shaped by folk song and hymn-like warmth. The journey then turns north with Sibelius’s Symphony No. 1 in E minor, a work that announces the arrival of a distinctive symphonic voice. Premiered in 1899, its brooding clarinet opening leads without pause into bold drama, while striking string and woodwind solos shape music alive with urgency and sweep. Though echoes of Tchaikovsky’s lyricism and Romantic intensity, the symphony speaks with unmistakable individuality, culminating in a hammering scherzo and a finale of stirring force.

Artists:

Ryan Bancroft - Conductor

Steffan Lloyd Owen - Baritone

Repertoire:

Ravel Le Tombeau de Couperin Vaughan-Williams Songs of TravelIntervalSibelius Symphony No.1
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > War and Peace

title: THE ROCK & ROLL MAGIC SHOW
description:
‘Will Leave you amazed and singing along… Don’t Miss Out!’

Arron was on Britain’s Got Talent… Now he wants REVENGE!

Join London’s West End’s Rockstar Magician Arron Jones, as he sticks it to Simon Cowell by performing a magic trick so face meltingly amazing it soars to #1 in the UK singles chart. Is it possible? Not without your help!

The Rock & Roll Magic Show is BURSTING at the seams with COMEDY, LIVE MUSIC and of course MAGIC, all performed by The Rock & Roll’s Greatest Magician himself.

Featuring Magical parodies of your favourite hits from Queen, Journey, Twisted Sister, Joan and the Jetts, Billy Joel and many more!

title: CHRISTOPHER HALL - PIZAZZ
description:
Showman, Jester, Attention Chaser, come with Chris on the ultimate hometown holiday night out.  From your 1.5-star hotel, he’ll give you a hilariously fun soirée and have you tucked up back in bed at a sensible time.

Christopher Hall, once first on the dancefloor and last on the night bus is now in his early mid 30s (very different to the late early's) and reflecting on his younger life-of-the-party self.  This theatrical showboy started out putting on shows in the front room for his parents then spent his early and late mid 20s have dance-offs in Soho’s gay bars and performing in musicals on cruise ships.   Background Singers featuring collabs with Sugababes, Natasha Bedingfield, Sophie Ellis Bextor and Shania Twain shot him to fame on Instagram and TikTok.  Now Chris is back IRL with his new, brilliantly hilarious stand-up show, with a high possibility of an outbreak of dancing.   This chorus boy takes centre stage for a fun, glamorous and unforgettable night, don't miss your chance to get your dose of ‘Pizazz’.

title: TEZ ILYAS: TEZ THINGS I HATE ABOUT YOU
description:
The grumpiest uncle in showbiz is back.

Fresh from travelling the world (and smashing it as a top husband and bunny-stepdad), the UK’s leading de-influencer returns with a brand-new stand-up show. Expect fresh jokes, furious rants, and ridiculous stories — all delivered with Tez’s trademark smart, silly, and subversive humour.

Star of: Man Like Mobeen (BBC Three), Live at the Apollo (BBC Two), Fighting Talk (BBC Radio 5 Live) and Sunday Times bestselling author of The Secret Diary of a British Muslim Aged 13 ¾.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > TEZ ILYAS: TEZ THINGS I HATE ABOUT YOU

title: SOOZ KEMPNER: IMPOSTER
description:
Award-winning comedian Sooz Kempner (Doom in Doctor Who’s Doom’s Day, The Now Show) has always felt a little like an imposter.

Except in one area: singing! She could always rely on THAT. But when Sooz's voice suddenly stopped working the way it always had before, she had to face her imposter syndrome head-on and find out once and for all: is it imposter syndrome, or is she truly an imposter? A stand-up show about finding your voice, silencing trolls and Princess Diana.

title: BBC NOW - NOW!
description:
DISCOVERY MOMENTUM EXPRESSION

This boldly imaginative programme, conducted by Simon Crawford-Phillips, brings three distinctive voices of contemporary music to the fore, framed by two major UK premieres and a world premiere. HK Gruber’s Short Stories from the Vienna Woods opens the evening with a series of sharply characterised musical illustrations, by turns playful, ironic and darkly expressive.At the heart of the concert is the world premiere of Erika Fox’s Viola Concerto, performed by celebrated soloist Lawrence Power. Richly exploratory in colour and gesture, the concerto places the viola centre stage, showcasing its expressive depth through Fox’s distinctive blend of lyricism and energetic drive.Finally, we have the UK premiere of Steven Mackey’s Concerto for Curved Space. A virtuosic and kaleidoscopic journey, the work bends rhythm, harmony and genre with irrepressible momentum, drawing the evening to a thrilling and expansive close.

Artists:

Simon Crawford Phillips - Conductor

Lawrence Power – Viola

Repertoire:

HK Gruber Short stories from the Vienna Woods [UK premiere]Erika Fox Viola Concerto [World premiere] INTERVAL Steven Mackey Concerto for Curved Space  [UK premiere]
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > BBC NOW - NOW!

title: MAMMA MIA!
description:
Join us at MAMMA MIA! and enjoy the ultimate feel-good factor at the world’s sunniest and most exhilarating smash-hit musical in Cardiff from 30 November 2026 for one week only!

Set on a Greek island paradise, a story of love, friendship and identity is cleverly told through the timeless songs of ABBA.

Sophie’s quest to discover the father she’s never known brings her mother face to face with three men from her distant romantic past on the eve of a wedding they’ll never forget.

This irresistibly funny show has been thrilling audiences all around the world and now there’s never been a better time to see this unforgettable musical in Cardiff.

Whatever age you are, you can’t help but have the time of your life at MAMMA MIA!

title: TCHAIKOVSKY’S NUTCRACKER
description:
MAGIC WONDER DELIGHTFUL

A festive, family friendly celebration of Seasonal classics, conducted by Luis Castillo Briceño. The evening opens with the magical Prelude from Humperdinck’s Hänsel und Gretel, where folk inspired melodies and glowing orchestral colours bring the fairy tale forest vividly to life. Frank Bridge’s sparkling Christmas Dance “Sir Roger de Coverley” follows, a whirlwind of variations on a much loved English tune traditionally played at Christmas celebrations, ending with a joyful nod to Auld Lang Syne.Benjamin Britten’s much loved Young Person’s Guide to the Orchestra offers an entertaining and illuminating tour of the orchestra, perfect for curious listeners of all ages. After the interval, Tchaikovsky’s irresistible Nutcracker Suite brings the concert to a close, overflowing with seasonal magic, dance, and unforgettable melodies.

Artists:

Luis Castillo-Briceno – Conductor

Steffan Rhodri – Narrator

Repertoire:

Humperdinck Hänsel und Gretel: Prelude Bridge Christmas Dance ""Sir Roger de Coverley"" Britten Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra INTERVAL Tchaikovsky Suite from Nutcracker
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > Tchaikovsky’s Nutcracker

title: CAMILLE O'SULLIVAN - LOVELETTER
description:
A love letter in song, Camille’s celebrates dear friend Shane MacGowan, Sinéad O’Connor, David Bowie, Radiohead, Brel, Nick Cave and more. Chameleon-like on stage, each song has a different character with its story to tell.

Camille with long-time collaborator Feargal Murray has created an intimate, heartfelt show with a little bit of joyful, mad chaos as her life unravels, capturing the honest response to those much loved and lost.

Voted “One of the top 25 performances ever on Later with Jools BBC” (Daily Telegraph) and "Queen of the Edinburgh Festival" (BBC), Camille toured with the Pogues, guested at Yoko Ono’s & Richard Thompson’s RFH Meltdown and original star of Olivier Award-winning La Clique. Previously an award-winning architect and painter, Camille was born in London to a French mother and Irish/English father, she grew up in a village in Cork, Ireland listening to her parents & sisters eclectic music collection that shaped her into the unique interpreter she is today.

The multi-award-winning singer has stunned audiences around the world with her five-star sell-out performances, including the Sydney Opera House, Royal Festival Hall, The Roundhouse, won the coveted Herald Angel award for her RSC solo performance ‘The Rape of Lucrece’ and recently an Architectural RIAI 2025 award.

Dark, sexy, fierce, amusing and mesmerizing, Camille transforms each song she performs into an intense, emotional, and theatrical experience. Expect joy and pure passion.

title: LEROY BRITO - HALFTIME
description:
As seen on Netflix's smash hit Baby Reindeer, with tour support slots for Rhod Gilbert and Mo Gilligan, Leroy Brito is back with his biggest and most ambitious show to date.

Halftime is Leroy's hilarious new hour of stand-up about turning 45, accepting that you're officially middle-aged, and stepping into your DILF era.

title: MATILDA THE MUSICAL
description:
Following the sellout success of its first tour in 2018/19, Matilda The Musical, the multi-award winning musical from the Royal Shakespeare Company, inspired by the beloved book by Roald Dahl, is heading back out on tour across the UK and Ireland.

In celebration of 15 years on stage since opening at the Courtyard Theatre in Stratford-upon-Avon, and following performances in 100 cities across 17 countries worldwide, Matilda now returns to Wales Millennium Centre.

With book by Dennis Kelly, music and lyrics by Tim Minchin and directed by Matthew Warchus, Matilda is the story of an extraordinary little girl who, armed with a vivid imagination and a sharp mind, dares to take a stand and change her own destiny.

Winner of over 100 international awards, including 24 for Best Musical, the ‘unique and unforgettable’ (Rolling Stone) global phenomenon, Matilda has now delighted over 12 million audiences worldwide.

title: BARRYLESQUE
description:
Hold on to your paper hats and fizz... This Christmas, Wales Millennium Centre is bringing you the most glamorous, most outrageous and most unmissably Welsh night out of the festive season.

Barrylesque is a drag cabaret spectacular that takes the beloved world of Gavin & Stacey and gives it the full Barry Island treatment. Featuring all your fave characters, reimagined as larger-than-life drag and burlesque performers for a night of live vocals, lip sync, comedy and chaos. Expect iconic catchphrases, outrageous characters, and a singalong finale that'll have the whole house on its feet. Tidy.

And maybe, just maybe, we might finally work out what happened on that fishing trip.

Part cabaret, part sitcom, entirely tongue-in-cheek, Barrylesque is the only Christmas gift worth giving this year.

Don't miss it. It's well lush.

title: THE MAGIC OF CHRISTMAS
description:
Celebrate the season with an uplifting evening of music as we bring you our Christmas Celebration concerts, filled with warmth, joy, and sparkle.

This festive programme brings together much‑loved seasonal favourites alongside orchestral showpieces that capture the magic of Christmas.

Perfect for families, friends, and all who enjoy marking Christmas through music, this concert offers a chance to pause, reflect, and rejoice.

Join us for an evening of festive cheer, heartfelt music, and the shared spirit of Christmas.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2026 > The Magic of Christmas

title: TCHAIKOVSKY 5
description:
This richly coloured concert is conducted by Principal Guest Conductor Jaime Martín and brings together music of atmosphere, virtuosity and symphonic power.

Grace Williams’ Sea Sketches opens the evening, capturing the character of the Welsh coastline in music of freshness, colour and flowing movement.

The focus then turns to harpist Mared Pugh‑Evans, soloist in Henriette Renié’s Harp Concerto, a deeply Romantic work shaped by long, sweeping melodies and brilliant, virtuosic passages for the harp.

After the interval, Tchaikovsky’s Symphony No. 5 charts a dramatic journey from struggle to hard‑won triumph, bringing the programme to an impassioned close.

Expressive Rich Destiny

Williams Sea Sketches (20')Henreitte Renie Harp Concerto (25')Interval Tchaikovsky Symphony No.5 (44')
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2027 > Tchaikovsky 5

title: STRAUSS & WAGNER
description:
Strauss & Wagner: two towering figures at the height of their expressive powers.

Richard Strauss’s Four Last Songs, composed in 1948, a year before his death, stand as one of the most luminous farewells in all music. Setting deeply contemplative texts, Strauss reflects on love, transience and release, drawing the listener into what he called “the magic circle of the night.”

After the interval, Wagner’s Tristan und Isolde: An Orchestral Passion reveals the composer at his most revolutionary. This sumptuous orchestral synthesis has long been cherished as a concert work in its own right, its lush romantic surface concealing a daring harmonic language that transformed music history from its opening bars.

Conducted by Anthony Hermus, the programme offers an immersive journey through two composers redefining the emotional and musical possibilities of their art.

PASSION FAREWELL LUMINOUS Strauss VIER LETZTE LIEDER (Four Last Songs) (24') Interval (20') Wagner arr. | trefn. Henk de Vlieger Tristan und Isolde: An Orchestral Passion (60')
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2027 > Strauss & Wagner

title: PARANORMAL ACTIVITY
description:
Direct from a sold out West End run.

James and Lou move from Chicago to London to escape their past, but they soon discover that places aren’t haunted, people are...

This thrilling West End hit is inspired by the iconic, hugely popular film series, Paranormal Activity, with a brand-new story live on stage. Now embarking on its first ever UK and Ireland tour after a sell-out London season, this chilling production promises to keep you on the edge of your seat from start to finish. Hold your nerve...

title: TRAINSPOTTING: THE MUSICAL
description:
Thirty years on from the film that changed everything, TRAINSPOTTING: THE MUSICAL has its world premiere.

Renton, Sick Boy, Begbie, Spud, Tommy, and Kelly, the industrial drug-crazed working-class heroes, are back.

In 1996, CHOOSE LIFE sounded like a challenge. Thirty years on, the landscape has shifted, but the hunger hasn’t. Addiction hasn’t disappeared - it’s shifted. It’s quieter now, more embedded. Less needle, more screen. Less escape, more repetition. The same restless search for something to fill the space.

The musical highlights the world we see changing around us, the obscene concentration of wealth, economic and political power in the hands of so few people. It addresses the modern culture of powerlessness. Art the singing-and-dancing Trojan horse that delivers direction and relevance.

This isn’t nostalgia, it’s closer than that. The past isn’t revisited - it lingers. The musical doesn’t tidy it. It sits with it. Listens to it. And lets it sing. New songs, classic songs, a musical like never before. Not for the easily confronted. It’s funny, loud, dynamic, and brings a dark dangerous energy never experienced before on the West End stage.

“I believe the musical has a bigger, loudly beating human heart than either the book or the film. People need to think about the world we’re living in, and we offer that inspection, but they also really need to sing their hearts out and laugh their heads off - it’s what being human is all about.” Irvine Welsh, April 2026

There are no clean answers here. Only incredible music and flashes of clarity, humour, and the uneasy sense of recognition.

CHOOSE LIFE.

Written by Irvine Welsh with Music & Lyrics by Stephen McGuinness & Irvine Welsh and Directed & Developed by Caroline Jay Ranger.
subcategory: Wales Millennium Centre > What's on > 2027 > Trainspotting: The Musical

title: CITIES OF SOUND: NEW YORK, BERLIN, VIENNA
description:
From Mozart’s classical brilliance to the bold energy of twentieth century modernism, this programme crackles with theatrical flair and rhythmic drive.

Conductor Anna Rakitina leads a richly varied evening that opens with a suite from Elena Langer’s Figaro Gets a Divorce, a witty and expressive sequel to Mozart’s operatic world. Mozart himself follows with the radiant Piano Concerto No. 21, its elegance and lyrical warmth brought to life by pianist Clare Hammond.

After the interval, George Antheil’s Jazz Symphony bursts with swagger and rhythmic bite, before Stravinsky’s Symphony in Three Movements delivers a bracing, propulsive finale.

BRILLIANCE THEATRE RHYTHM

Elena Langer Figaro Gets a Divorce  (20') Mozart Piano Concerto No.21  (26') Interval George Anthiel Jazz Symphony  Stravinsky Symphony in Three Movements  (22')

title: THE FULL MONTY
description:
Sometimes you have to bare everything!

Celebrating the 30th Anniversary of the iconic film that took British cinema by storm, The Everyman Theatre, Cheltenham and Buxton Opera House in association with Mark Goucher and David Pugh present The Full Monty, live on stage. Back by popular demand, the boys are returning with a major national tour!

There are no heroes in Simon Beaufoy’s heartfelt play, just a group of lads trying to regain their dignity and pride. This fast and funny play is still very much of our time, as again we are hit by a cost-of-living crisis.
